contest_compliance_notes: Manifest must avoid hidden side channels and must record every charged byte source.
Planning artifact only; exact archive custody is required before promotion.
entries.next_patch: Define a charged atom manifest that separates syntax bytes, decoder bytes, and frozen input streams before renderer integration.
entries.summary: Turn the charged mask grammar and ego-foveation notes into a byte-accounted local atom manifest before any renderer integration.
entries.title: Charged mask grammar atoms

contest_compliance_notes: Local byte analysis only; exact archive eval is required before promotion.
Do not reference provider workspaces or state files from the manifest.
entries.next_patch: Use `experiments/sweep_owv3_byte_plan.py --manifest-only` as the fast planning preflight, then run the full sweep only with authoritative CUDA sensitivity custody.
entries.summary: Use certified component-sensitivity artifacts to plan renderer payload byte allocation without reading provider queues or launching eval work.
entries.title: Component sensitivity byte allocator

contest_compliance_notes: Smoke and trend artifacts are development evidence only.
Renderer payload changes need byte-closed archives and exact CUDA replay.
entries.next_patch: Add a deterministic CUDA smoke-matrix runner and block archive wiring until trend metadata proves non-regression.
entries.summary: Promote Cool-Chic and C3 renderer prototypes from local wiring smoke to a controlled CUDA trend gate before archive integration.
entries.title: Cool-Chic C3 renderer trend gate

contest_compliance_notes: No correction atom can promote from proxy or byte-only evidence.
Every runtime-applied atom must be charged inside the archive.
entries.next_patch: Wire signed correction readiness reports into a byte-closed archive builder candidate; exact dispatch remains blocked until candidate diff and archive preflight prove charged atom consumption.
entries.summary: Make AMR-style and pixel-diff correction atoms no-op-resistant, signed by component traces, and fail-closed before dispatch.
entries.title: Engineered correction atom gate

contest_compliance_notes: Fridrich/UNIWARD maps are optimizer feedback only until consumed by charged archive bytes.
No prior UNIWARD result promotes without proving the computed payload reached inflate.
entries.next_patch: Feed detector-cost manifests into the stack orchestrator as optimizer feedback only; keep archive builds blocked until charged atoms alter a byte-closed candidate.
entries.summary: Use UNIWARD/Fridrich-style detector-aware costs as allocation feedback for charged residual atoms and HNeRV section transforms.
entries.title: Fridrich inverse-steg allocator

contest_compliance_notes: Byte forensics only; no archive is eligible for dispatch until exact replay gates pass.
Candidate manifests must preserve archive and member hashes.
entries.next_patch: Use `tac.archive_byte_profile.build_candidate_diff_manifest` as the mandatory no-op control for future HNeRV repack candidates before archive preflight or exact CUDA eval.
entries.summary: Make public HNeRV payload anatomy drive deterministic repack follow-ups from section bytes and provenance instead of prose.
entries.title: HNeRV payload scorecard follow-ups

contest_compliance_notes: No provider state, provider logs, or live job identifiers belong in this manifest.
Stack deltas remain planning evidence until exact archive custody exists.
entries.next_patch: Add a typed local manifest that records each stack stage and its byte contract without dispatch metadata.
entries.summary: Record representation, prediction, quantization, hyperprior, arithmetic, and pack stages as typed local contracts before stacking experiments.
entries.title: Joint stack contract manifest

contest_compliance_notes: Side information must be charged inside the packed payload.
Inflate must remain scorer-free and deterministic.
entries.next_patch: Port static-model arithmetic pack and unpack helpers onto HNeRV latent-correction sidecar bytes with a parity-first fixture.
entries.summary: Reuse static arithmetic-coding helpers for latent-correction sidecar bytes after proving byte-for-byte decode parity on fixtures.
entries.title: Latent sidecar arithmetic terminal

contest_compliance_notes: No training run or archive can promote without the L2 clearance artifact.
Learned mask bytes and decoder state must be charged inside the archive.
entries.next_patch: Add a dry-run readiness command that checks L2 clearance, runtime dispatch, dependency closure, and exact archive-builder availability.
entries.summary: Recover the NeRV mask codec lane as a guarded build-only path until L2 clearance, parser closure, and pose provenance are present.
entries.title: NeRV mask L2 readiness

contest_compliance_notes: Sensitivity is optimizer feedback, not score evidence.
Production dispatch needs exact source archive identity, CUDA device provenance, and component_sensitivity_map_certification_v1 metadata.
entries.next_patch: Wire certified sensitivity maps into a concrete OWV3 candidate builder and keep exact CUDA eval as the only promotion path.
entries.summary: Make the PR106 water-fill repack lane refuse stub, stale, or mismatched sensitivity metadata before production dispatch.
entries.title: Omega-W-V3 real sensitivity gate

contest_compliance_notes: Proxy self-consistency cannot unlock exact-eval dispatch by itself.
Production readiness proves sister archives are anchored to the same PR106 payload, manifests match archive paths and bytes, and stacked dispatch blockers remain explicit until exact CUDA auth eval.
entries.next_patch: Implement one CUDA scorer-backed yshift or latent search mode, then feed its exact CUDA artifact back into the stack gate.
entries.summary: Coordinate latent, yshift, LRL1, and stacked PR106 sidechannels through one dry-run gate so sister archives cannot drift or dispatch from proxy-only modes.
entries.title: PR106 sidechannel stack gate

contest_compliance_notes: Static custody and runtime-contract audits are not score evidence.
No PR91/HPM1 candidate may dispatch until the HPM1 runtime consumes charged bytes without uncharged sidecars or fallback.
entries.next_patch: Produce a real `pr91_hpm1_decode_reencode_parity_v1` report from the HPM1 decoder; only then can the readiness audit narrow to exact CUDA eval.
entries.summary: Keep the public PR91 HPM1 mask stream visible as a high-EV categorical rate signal while its HPAC device contract, full decode, and re-encode parity remain fail-closed.
entries.title: PR91 HPM1 runtime contract

contest_compliance_notes: Sidecars must stay forbidden and every atom plan must preserve source hashes.
Candidate archives need exact replay custody before promotion.
entries.next_patch: Create a tracked PR95-family atom planner with a no-op-resistant fixture and candidate-member diff report before any archive rebuild.
entries.summary: Turn PR95-family residual atom notes into a tracked no-op-resistant candidate-member diff workflow.
entries.title: PR95 residual atom planner

contest_compliance_notes: RAFT outputs are profile feedback until charged bytes consume them in inflate.
Small geometry errors require exact CUDA component gates before promotion.
entries.next_patch: Add a RAFT readiness command that emits a pose-disagreement artifact and refuses inflate/runtime modes without that proof.
entries.summary: Use RAFT and radial ego-motion as pose-sidecar or TTO-initializer feedback only after disagreement and runtime-consumption proofs exist.
entries.title: RAFT radial pose readiness

contest_compliance_notes: Wavelet coefficients are charged side information unless generated by fixed contest code.
Exact CUDA archive eval is required before ranking any wavelet residual transform.
entries.next_patch: Claim lane dispatch, run exact CUDA auth eval on the byte-negative offline WR01 half-strength candidate, then harvest and adjudicate before promotion.
entries.summary: Use wavelet bases as compact residual or mask-side correction coordinates, not as an unchecked replacement for the current HNeRV anchor.
entries.title: Wavelet residual basis gate
